content.full_text:
GUS J. SKROPOS
g"r-r*8r-.,
city of Ontorlo
P.O. Box 1607
Ontorlo. CA 91762
tD#w2.#4
January 17, 1992
Mr. Jay Greenwood
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
Dear Mr. Greenwood:
'f
2i
I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of
January 16, 1992 .
Yesterday, I indicated to you that I currently have an
active campaign committee and bank account which f opened
and established prior to my electj.on to the ontario City
Council in November of 1990. I told you that since the
election I have done some fundraising to extinguish a
campaign debt and raise some funds for expenses during my
term of office. 
You, as well as others in your organiz-
ation, indicated that this was no problem utilizing 
the
existing committee.
I inguired about the mechanism I should use to raise funds
for my re-election campaign in 1994. Specifically, I wanted
to know if I should open a new bank account and committee,
or, amend the existing committee and account via forms 501
and 502. You inquired and told me that f should use the
existing organization and account and file the amended forms
indicating the 1994 election date. You said that this would
be appropriate if I was not carryj-ng a debt from my last
campaign.
Per your advice, I am enclosing the amended forms 501 and
502 you requested.
I would like to express my appreciation for your assistance
in clearing up my confusion on this matter.
%
Nol Printed ot Governr€nl Expensa

sections.question_synthetic: Can an elected official use an existing campaign committee and bank account for a re-election campaign in 1994, or should a new committee and account be opened?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence from a city council member confirming advice already given by telephone. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document appears to be a follow-up letter enclosing amended forms rather than a formal FPPC advice letter. No clear question and answer structure visible.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can an elected official use an existing campaign committee and bank account for a re-election campaign in 1994, or should a new committee and account be opened?

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95812-0807 Dear Mr. Greenwood: 'f 2i I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation of January 16, 1992 . Yesterday, I indicated to you that I currently have an active campaign committee and bank account which f opened and established prior to my electj.on to the ontario City Council in November of 1990. I told you that since the election I have done some fundraising to extinguish a campaign debt and raise some funds for expenses during my term of office. You, as well as others in your organiz- ation, indicated that this was no problem utilizing the existing committee. I inguired about the mechanism I should use to raise funds for my re-election campaign in 1994. Specifically, I wanted to know if I should open a new bank account and committee, or, amend the existing committee and account via forms 501 and 502. You inquired and told me that f should use the existing organization and account and file the amended forms indicating the 1994 election date. You said that this would be appropriate if I was not carryj-ng a debt from my last campaign. Per your advice, I am enclosing the amended forms 501 and 502 you requested. I would like to express my appreciation for your assistance in clearing up my confusion on this matter. % Nol Printed ot Governr€nl Expensa
embedding.summary: Gus Skropos writes to confirm a telephone conversation with FPPC staff regarding whether to use his existing campaign committee and bank account for his 1994 re-election campaign. He indicates that FPPC advised him to amend the existing committee and account using forms 501 and 502, provided he carries no campaign debt from the previous election.
